headline: NYT Claims Adam Back Is Satoshi Nakamoto, Bitcoin's Pseudonymous Founder
summary: The New York Times has published an investigation asserting that Blockstream CEO Adam Back is Satoshi Nakamoto, the pseudonymous creator of Bitcoin. Back is a cryptographer and cypherpunk pioneer whose Hashcash proof-of-work system directly preceded and influenced Bitcoin's design. If substantiated, this resolves one of tech's longest-running identity mysteries, though previous Satoshi claims have failed scrutiny and Back himself has denied being Nakamoto.
oci_implication: This story has negligible direct impact on OCI strategy — it is a media narrative, not a technical or market event. Monitor only if regulatory or institutional Bitcoin developments follow that could affect enterprise blockchain or digital asset infrastructure demand.
